title: SOC 2 C1 — Confidential Information Disposal and Secure Destruction Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOC 2 C1.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 C1.1 (Confidentiality) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.8.29: SOC 2 C1 — Confidential Information Disposal and Secure Destruction Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.8.29' running on sourcetype disposal:cert and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="disposal:cert"` (asset_id, method, cert_number, destroyed_ts, witness); `index=endpoint` BitLocker or secure erase tool logs
app: Splunk DB Connect, vendor APIs (Iron Mountain, secure erase tools) via HEC
spl:
index=compliance sourcetype="disposal:cert" earliest=-365d
| eval destroyed=strptime(destroyed_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left asset_id [
    search index=cmdb sourcetype="cmdb:asset" earliest=-400d
    | fields asset_id, retired_date, classification
    | eval retired=strptime(retired_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
  ]
| where retired>relative_time(destroyed,"+30d@d")
| table asset_id, classification, retired, destroyed, cert_number
description: C1 addresses confidential information. Tracking destruction certificates, crypto-shredding events, and DLP “secure delete” workflows demonstrates media and logical disposal met policy when assets retired or contracts ended.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cmdb, compliance, endpoint and sourcetypes cmdb:asset, disposal:cert. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Flip logic per policy: flag retirements without cert within 30 days; (2) restrict `classification=confidential` scope; (3) integrate ITAD vendor feed; (4) legal hold exceptions via `legal_hold_assets.csv`; (5) annual sample for auditors with cert PDF links (out of band). Indexes required: `index=cmdb`, `index=compliance`, `index=endpoint`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cmdb:asset`, `sourcetype=disposal:cert`.
visualization: Table (late disposal), Single value (assets past SLA), Bar by classification.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect, vendor APIs (Iron Mountain, secure erase tools) via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="disposal:cert"` (asset_id, method, cert_number, destroyed_ts, witness); `index=endpoint` BitLocker or secure erase tool logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Flip logic per policy: flag retirements without cert within 30 days; (2) restrict `classification=confidential` scope; (3) integrate ITAD vendor feed; (4) legal hold exceptions via `legal_hold_assets.csv`; (5) annual sample for auditors with cert PDF links (out of band).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="disposal:cert" earliest=-365d
| eval destroyed=strptime(destroyed_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left asset_id [
    search index=cmdb sourcetype="cmdb:asset" earliest=-400d
    | fields asset_id, retired_date, classification
    | eval retired=strptime(retired_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
  ]
| where retired>relative_time(destroyed,"+30d@d")
| table asset_id, classification, retired, destroyed, cert_number
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 C1 — Confidential Information Disposal and Secure Destruction Evidence** — C1 addresses confidential information. Tracking destruction certificates, crypto-shredding events, and DLP “secure delete” workflows demonstrates media and logical disposal met policy when assets retired or contracts ended.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="disposal:cert"` (asset_id, method, cert_number, destroyed_ts, witness); `index=endpoint` BitLocker or secure erase tool logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect, vendor APIs (Iron Mountain, secure erase tools) via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: disposal:cert. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype="disposal:cert", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **destroyed** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where retired>relative_time(destroyed,"+30d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: asset_id, cert_number, classification, destroyed, retired.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (late disposal), Single value (assets past SLA), Bar by classification.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for c1 addresses confidential information. tracking destruction certificates, crypto-shredding events, and dlp “secure delete” workflows demonstrates media and logical disposal met policy when assets r. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
